flavorText: Diviners pierce fate and manipulate outcomes with prophetic insight.

features.description: The gold and time to copy Divination spells into your spellbook are halved.
After each long rest, roll two d20s and record the numbers. You can replace any attack roll, saving throw, or ability check made by a creature you can see with one of these foretelling rolls.
When you cast a Divination spell of 2nd level or higher, recover one expended spell slot of a lower level.
As a bonus action, gain one benefit for 10 minutes: Darkvision 60 feet, Ethereal Sight, Comprehend Languages, or See Invisibility. Usable once per short rest.
Your Portent feature now grants three d20 rolls after each long rest.

diviner-divination-savant.summary: Your spellbook growth encourages a playstyle built around information, foresight, and prediction.

diviner-portent.summary: Store foreseen d20 results and replace future rolls with them at the exact moment fate needs a push.
diviner-portent.description: Portent is one of the most distinctive wizard subclass features in the game. It should feel like loaded future d20 results and stored rolls: planning ahead, remembering the stored numbers, and deciding exactly which roll matters enough to replace.

diviner-expert-divination.summary: Divination magic starts feeding your spell economy back, rewarding the subclass for playing to theme.

diviner-the-third-eye.summary: Open extra supernatural senses when information is more valuable than raw damage.

diviner-greater-portent.summary: You see even more of the future, making your stored-roll control harder for the table to ignore.
